highlights: protocol.json and launcher agree on append-only JSONL plus required_fields for proposals
proposals.jsonl is headerless one-object-per-line JSONL
proposal records satisfy required_fields but evidence/timestamp field types are inconsistent across records
adoption is not ledgered; bundle/result traceability is instructed but not validated
all peer_review records omit required evidence field despite protocol required_fields contract
target_item_id, target_commander, and verdict are operationally necessary but undocumented
review-to-proposal linkage works today via target_item_id
review-to-improvement-to-adoption forward trace is not contractually defined
improvement records inherit only generic required_fields; no improvement-specific schema exists
launcher says write improvements but does not require linkage back to review/proposal or forward to consensus
empty/malformed-line tolerance exists only as protocol guidance; consumers mainly count lines
improvement provenance is too weak for deterministic adoption traceability
protocol defines workflow stage consensus but not promotion criteria from improvements
actual consensus records rely on undocumented verdict and based_on fields
workflow includes adopt stage but no adoption ledger exists
bundle example in commander doc omits adopted_consensus_item_ids/source_refs required by launcher prompt
broadcasts trace to consensus via optional consensus_item_id, not a required protocol field
protocol required_fields are too generic to enforce end-to-end linkage
launcher requires adopted consensus item_ids/source_refs in bundle/results, but docs and manifest examples drift
adoption proof currently requires reading bundle outputs, not the collaboration bus alone

synthesis.overall_assessment: Collaboration-bus bootstrap is sound (precreated ledgers, append-only JSONL guidance, stable item_id intent), but per-ledger semantics are under-specified and end-to-end adoption traceability is not fully auditable from the bus alone.
positives: stampede.sh precreates proposals, reviews, improvements, consensus, and broadcasts ledgers in append mode and writes protocol.json before commander work begins
proposal and broadcast/consensus records demonstrate organic cross-ledger linkage through item_id, based_on, and consensus_item_id
monitor safely counts ledger activity without mutating files
blockers: No formal adoption ledger or equivalent append-only proof for the adopt workflow stage
Consensus promotion criteria and required linkage fields are undocumented; actual records depend on ad hoc verdict/based_on fields
Commander bundle documentation omits adopted_consensus_item_ids/source_refs even though launcher prompt requires them
majors: reviews.jsonl records currently violate protocol required_fields by omitting evidence
protocol required_fields are generic and do not specify per-ledger linkage fields such as target_item_id, based_on, or consensus_item_id
improvements and broadcasts can be counted but not fully validated by existing consumers, so malformed semantics may pass unnoticed
bundle/result adoption traceability is not enforced by runtime validation
minors: proposal evidence and timestamp field types vary across commanders
cross-commander review coverage is currently asymmetric
synthesis.traceability_verdict: Partial: backward references exist, but forward proof from review/improvement to adopted bundle output is not guaranteed by the protocol.
